heroByLens.overview: My work sits between institutional AI enablement, media and web systems, DIY making, and digital humanities.
heroByLens.builder: I move between infrastructure, interface, and object-making, using each one to pressure-test the others.
heroByLens.leader: I build programs, platforms, and operating structures that let institutions use technology deliberately instead of reactively.
heroByLens.scholar: The practice joins technical implementation with questions about media history, publishing systems, and knowledge work.
heroByLens.hiring: This is a portfolio of strategic delivery across academic technology, AI enablement, media systems, and cross-functional leadership.

copy.overview: Institutional AI enablement, academic technology operations, and platform design inside university systems.
copy.builder: Workflow design, policy translation, tooling, and interfaces treated as one build surface.
copy.leader: Programs and systems that need governance, coordination, and resilient operational structure.
copy.scholar: How institutions absorb new technical media and turn them into administrative and pedagogical practice.
copy.hiring: Evidence of large-scale responsibility where strategy and implementation have to meet.

copy.overview: Small tools, hardware/software prototypes, and utility work that keep the larger practice grounded.
copy.builder: Experiments where I can move quickly, test ideas, and learn through constraints instead of presentation.
copy.leader: A live making practice that sharpens implementation judgment and keeps management close to materials.
copy.scholar: Prototype work as method: a way to think with systems by building and revising artifacts.
copy.hiring: Shows hands-on fluency rather than leadership detached from actual tools.

copy.overview: Essays and notes on software, media, publishing, digital humanities, and technology as culture.
copy.builder: Writing used to clarify implementation decisions, technical history, and the shape of the work.
copy.leader: Public thinking that explains not just what was built, but why the framing matters.
copy.scholar: A writing practice grounded in software studies, digital humanities, and the history of the web.
copy.hiring: Signals clear communication, conceptual range, and a durable point of view.

constellations.title: Teaching media systems
constellations.reason: Rooms, templates, and metadata workflows all belong to the same teaching-media stack rather than sitting as isolated projects.

constellations.title: Publishing between systems
constellations.reason: The publication, the bridge, and the plugin all treat publishing as something that moves across platforms, formats, and interfaces.

constellations.title: Structured data in public
constellations.reason: Each artifact turns messy or distributed sources into structured data that can be queried, reused, or interpreted.

tracks.title: Systems
tracks.deck: AI enablement, academic technology, media systems, operational design, and institutional tooling.

tracks.title: Builds
tracks.deck: DIY hardware/software, prototypes, utilities, and bench-scale experiments.

tracks.title: Studio
tracks.deck: Aesthetic technology work, interfaces as form, and experiments in data and computation.

tracks.title: Writing
tracks.deck: Digital Born, digital humanities, software studies, publishing history, notes, and essays.

systems.overview: AI enablement, academic technology, media operations, and service design.
systems.builder: Systems where policy, workflow, tooling, and interface decisions all have to line up.
systems.leader: Institution-scale delivery work across teams, programs, and technical platforms.
systems.scholar: Institutional systems understood as media environments and knowledge infrastructure.
systems.hiring: Strategic technical leadership with operational depth and delivery accountability.

builds.overview: Small tools, hardware/software experiments, and objects made to learn by making.
builds.builder: A live prototyping practice focused on utility, constraints, and iteration.
builds.leader: Hands-on making that keeps larger leadership work grounded in real implementation.
builds.scholar: Artifacts used as a way to think through technical culture, not just solve tasks.
builds.hiring: Signals direct technical fluency and the ability to move from concept to working thing.

studio.overview: Data aesthetics, interface objects, and controlled generative structures.
studio.builder: A place to test layout, interaction, and visual systems as technical material.
studio.leader: Design judgment carried by someone who also understands systems and production constraints.
studio.scholar: Technology approached as medium, not just instrument.
studio.hiring: Shows taste, experimentation, and the ability to make technical work feel intentional.

writing.overview: Writing that sits between digital humanities, software studies, web history, and practice.
writing.builder: Essays and notes that clarify implementation, tooling, and technical history.
writing.leader: Public thinking that turns complex work into language other people can act on.
writing.scholar: A writing practice rooted in software, media, archives, and publishing.
writing.hiring: Signals communication range, conceptual rigor, and editorial discipline.

work.overview: Filter by track or theme to move across systems work, builds, studio artifacts, and writing.
work.builder: Use the archive to find tools, prototypes, workflows, and implementation patterns.
work.leader: Use the archive to find institution-scale work, cross-functional systems, and service design patterns.
work.scholar: Use the archive to trace themes across writing, building, and systems practice.
work.hiring: Use the archive to find the strongest evidence of fit for leadership, strategy, and delivery.

about.overview: A builder-leader-scholar practice organized across institutional systems, bench work, studio experiments, and writing.
about.builder: A technical practice that moves between platforms, objects, and publishing systems instead of staying in one lane.
about.leader: Leadership grounded in implementation, service design, and systems that have to hold up in real use.
about.scholar: A practice shaped by media history, digital humanities, software studies, and the material life of technical systems.
about.hiring: A concise frame for role fit, scope, and the through-line connecting leadership, making, and writing.

now.overview: A current-state snapshot across institutional systems work, active experiments, and writing.
now.builder: What is actively being made, tested, and revised right now across tools, interfaces, and prototypes.
now.leader: Where operational attention is currently focused across institutional AI, academic technology, and service design.
now.scholar: The questions and lines of inquiry currently shaping the archive and the practice around it.
now.hiring: A fast view of current priorities, current domains, and where attention is actively invested.

contact.overview: The best routes for reaching me about systems work, collaboration, writing, or roles.
contact.builder: Reach out about tools, prototypes, publishing systems, and practical technical work.
contact.leader: Reach out about institutional AI, academic technology, media systems, or organizational design.
contact.scholar: Reach out about digital humanities, software studies, media history, and public writing.
contact.hiring: Reach out about leadership roles, advisory work, and strategic technical collaboration.
